method_atom: particle-based ab-initio molecular-dynamics simulation of a large collection of interacting units, used to show that a densely packed regular (body-centred-cubic) arrangement of point-like units reorganises under increasing compression into anisotropic rod-like and layered structures; the structural phase transition proceeds through an intermediate zigzag configuration of elongated units formed by the pairwise merging of neighbouring units
object_atom: atomic nuclei in the ultra-dense matter of collapsing supernova cores, forming nuclear "pasta" phases such as triangular lattices of rod-like nuclei and layered slab-like structures
brief_reason: The compression-driven reorganisation of packed units from a cubic lattice into rod-like/layered structures via merging, computed by particle-based molecular dynamics, is the transferable method; the atomic-nuclei-in-supernova-cores system is the nuclear-astrophysics-specific object.
